title: Kent Custody Suites: What Happens After Arrest
metaTitle: Kent Custody After Arrest | Process Explained
metaDescription: What typically happens after arrest at a Kent police station — booking in, legal advice, interview, and outcomes. Sourced overview.
contentHtml:
Introduction

After arrest in Kent, a person is usually taken to a custody suite. This article summarises the typical stages — not outcomes for any individual case. For a full index of guides, see our Kent police custody resources.

Booking in

The custody officer records details and explains rights under PACE. The detainee should be told they may receive free legal advice (GOV.UK).

Legal advice

A solicitor may attend before interview. See PACE Code C and custody time limits.

Interview and outcome

Outcomes may include charge, release under investigation, or no further action.

Further reading

If a loved one is arrested · Who can instruct a solicitor
author: Robert Cashman
